description: PDF/DOCX report export with branding, Guided Mode enhancements (floating glossary, guardrails, sensitivity detection), Workspace/Collaboration features (projects, audit log, versioning, onboarding), UX polish (empty states, keep-alive workflow), and SUPABASE_SETUP.md update with new tables.
steps: Step 1 - Update utils/report_generator.py: (a) Add generate_pdf_report(df, title, branding=None, ai_summary=None) using fpdf2. Creates a multi-page PDF with: cover page (logo if branding provided, title, date), executive summary page, data overview table, key charts (rendered as images via plotly .to_image() or as table fallback if kaleido unavailable), and data quality section. Branding dict has keys: logo_bytes, company_name, primary_color, secondary_color. (b) Add generate_docx_report(df, title, branding=None, ai_summary=None) using python-docx. Creates a Word doc with: title page, executive summary, statistics tables, and section headers. Apply branding colors to headings if provided. (c) Both functions return bytes suitable for st.download_button.

Step 2 - Update pages/7_Report_Generator.py: (a) Add format selector: HTML (existing), PDF, DOCX. (b) Wire PDF/DOCX buttons to the new generator functions. (c) Add a 'Report Branding' expander in the sidebar or config section: file_uploader for logo (PNG/JPG), text inputs for company name, color pickers for primary/secondary colors. Store in session_state['report_branding']. Pass branding to report generators.

Step 3 - Update pages/0_Guided_Analysis.py: (a) Add a floating glossary: a persistent expander or popover button (use st.popover if available, otherwise st.expander in sidebar) labeled '?' containing searchable definitions of key analytics terms (correlation, p-value, outlier, IQR, regression, R-squared, etc. - at least 15 terms). Display throughout all stages. (b) Add responsible analysis guardrails that trigger contextually: if correlation > 0.85 is shown, display a 'Correlation does not equal Causation' warning. If cleaning would remove >30% of rows, show a warning before proceeding. If dataset has <30 rows, show a small sample warning. In Stage 2, add a 'data authorization checkbox' (already exists - verify it works). (c) Add sensitivity detection: at the start of Stage 3, call detect_sensitive_columns(). If any are found, show a persistent st.warning banner listing the columns and reasons. Offer a 'Mask these columns' button that calls mask_column() for each.

Step 4 - Create utils/workspace.py: Database-backed workspace features (all with graceful fallback when DB not configured): (a) save_project(name, description) inserts into dp_projects table. (b) list_projects() returns all projects. (c) get_project(project_id) returns project details. (d) delete_project(project_id). (e) log_action(project_id, action_type, description, details=None) inserts into dp_audit_log. (f) list_audit_log(project_id=None) returns audit entries. (g) save_dataset_version(project_id, dataset_name, df, version_note) inserts into dp_dataset_versions. (h) list_dataset_versions(project_id=None) returns version history. (i) restore_dataset_version(version_id) loads a specific version. All use the same (ok, payload) pattern as utils/database.py.

Step 5 - Update pages/9_Cloud_Workspace.py: Add new tabs: (a) 'Projects' tab: create project form (name, description), list existing projects as cards, set active project. (b) 'Audit Log' tab: display audit log entries filtered by project, with timestamp, action type, description. (c) 'Dataset Versions' tab: list versions for active project, show version number/timestamp/note, 'Restore' button for each. (d) 'Onboarding' tab: 5-step checklist (Upload data, Run profiling, Clean data, Generate insights, Create a report). Track completion in session_state['onboarding_complete']. Hide/collapse when all 5 done.

Step 6 - Update SUPABASE_SETUP.md: Add SQL for new tables: dp_projects (id, name, description, created_at, updated_at), dp_audit_log (id, project_id FK, action_type, description, details jsonb, created_at), dp_dataset_versions (id, project_id FK, dataset_name, version_number, data_csv text, version_note, created_at). Add indexes, RLS policies (same permissive anon pattern as existing tables). Add ALTER TABLE statements at the end for users upgrading from the previous schema (use IF NOT EXISTS pattern).

Step 7 - Update app.py: (a) Add all new session state keys in init_all_session_state or at the top of app.py: 'onboarding_complete' (dict with 5 bool keys), 'active_project_id', 'report_branding', 'dashboard_filters', 'dashboard_charts'. (b) Add the new pages to the sidebar navigation list. (c) Update the main page feature cards to mention new capabilities (Data Profiling, Dashboard, PDF/DOCX reports).

Step 8 - Add .github/workflows/keep-alive.yml: A GitHub Actions cron workflow that pings the deployed Streamlit app URL once daily to prevent Streamlit Cloud from spinning it down. Use a simple curl command. Schedule: daily at 6am UTC. Include a comment explaining purpose.

Step 9 - Add empty states using render_empty_state() from utils/styles.py: Go through pages/10_Data_Profiling.py and pages/11_Dashboard.py and ensure that when no data is loaded, they show a designed empty state (icon, headline, explanation, CTA button to upload page) instead of just st.info or st.warning.
acceptance_criteria: SUPABASE_SETUP.md contains CREATE TABLE for dp_projects, dp_audit_log, dp_dataset_versions
python -c 'from utils.workspace import save_project, list_projects, log_action, save_dataset_version, list_dataset_versions, restore_dataset_version; print("OK")' passes
python -c 'from utils.report_generator import generate_pdf_report, generate_docx_report; print("OK")' passes
verification: python -m py_compile utils/report_generator.py && python -m py_compile utils/workspace.py && python -m py_compile pages/7_Report_Generator.py && python -m py_compile pages/0_Guided_Analysis.py && python -m py_compile pages/9_Cloud_Workspace.py && python -m py_compile app.py

python -c "from utils.workspace import save_project, list_projects, log_action, save_dataset_version; from utils.report_generator import generate_pdf_report, generate_docx_report; print('All new features importable')"
grep -q 'dp_projects' SUPABASE_SETUP.md && grep -q 'dp_audit_log' SUPABASE_SETUP.md && grep -q 'dp_dataset_versions' SUPABASE_SETUP.md && echo 'SQL tables present'
